propositions: Field data showed remediation-eligible plan reviews producing zero novelty drops, ambiguous between a healthy precision-first scorer and a silently failing scorer whose fail-safe returns zero drops on error.

The live eval runner cannot execute the plan-review-novelty prompt because run_case only dispatches criterion ids, code-review prompt ids, and three hardcoded agentic reviewers, so run_eval raises ValueError per case.
A local probe called score_novelty live over reconstructed advisory/surfaced finding pairs from all post-2026-07-12 remediation-eligible sidecars: 34 pairs probed, 0 errors, 0 empty maps.
The scorer is healthy: 92 of 92 same-norm_id carryovers scored below the novelty threshold and novel findings scored high, satisfying the decision rule for a non-defective scorer.

The zero-drop premise was a measurement artifact from a parser that silently skipped JSON-encoded-string REVIEW_RESULT events; corrected parsing shows the production floor was actually narrowing 33 of 35 eligible reviews.
The remaining real gap is that BLOCK-loop rounds are never eligible because signed is false, tracked by a separate story.
